title: Create a contact in CRM from outlook email received
excerpt: We use Outlook for email.  When we receive a prospect email in outlook is there a way to create a contact in the CRM from outlook?  Thank you

posts.author: sskinney35 (Shari Kinney)
posts.cookedHtml: We use Outlook for email. When we receive a prospect email in outlook is there a way to create a contact in the CRM from outlook? Thank you

posts.author: sachinkarma (sachin kumar karma)

posts.author: ByronGriffin (ByronGriffin)
posts.cookedHtml: Of course, we can implement solutions and integrate both systems, such as Outlook for email, to create all contacts in CRM. We would like to receive the full scope of work for this project and how we can contact you to discuss details.

posts.author: mwalter (Matt Walter)
posts.cookedHtml:
In the CRM board you want the emails to update, click on the three dots in the upper right corner, select “settings” and select “create items via email.” Copy the address, and add the address to your address book in outlook and identify it as the CRM Board.

When you receive an email from a prospect, forward the email to the CRM Board using the address you have saved. When you do, change the subject line to be whatever you want it to be on the item on the CRM Board (probably the prospect name).

This will create an item on your board, with the email automatically added to the updates section of that item.
